independence_class: same GEO record; multiple cancer-type subsets, not fully independent multi-study validation
current_status: already analyzed with leave-dataset-out transfer, sensitivity/QC, and cancer-type downstream benchmark
biological_question: Do fibroblast-state compositions encode cancer-context differences while preserving marker gradients?
limitations: Useful but insufficient as the only biological validation because all subsets come from one GEO study and HCC remains QC-sensitive.
recommended_next_action: Keep as main cancer case study, but explicitly pair with at least one non-cancer external fibroblast dataset.

disease_context: human dermal fibroblast collagen scar/strain perturbation
submission_role: promote now as independent non-cancer validation
independence_class: independent GEO study; separate disease/perturbation context from GSE246215
current_status: already prepared and validated with leave-dataset-out plus leave-donor-out transfer
biological_question: Are fibroblast-state gradients transferable to a non-cancer scar/strain context?
endpoint: Donor-level state composition, leave-donor-out transfer, and treatment/strain gradient where labels are available.
limitations: Human3 hash groups lack public treatment mapping, so Human3 should support donor robustness only.
recommended_next_action: Promote to external validation panel and rerun selected-NB/transfer summary if final selector changes the default.

independence_class: independent GEO study; non-cancer disease context
current_status: fibroblast candidates and final transfer outputs available locally
biological_question: Does FibroDynMix recover ECM-remodeling/inflammatory/resident-state shifts in hypertrophic scar?
endpoint: Disease versus control pseudobulk composition and module score validation.
limitations: Current module result is positive-direction but small donor/sample count; treat as external validation, not clinical diagnostic evidence.
recommended_next_action: Integrate into FibroDynMix manuscript as the first independent non-cancer dataset and regenerate concise state-composition/gradient panels.

disease_context: keloid, normal scar, and normal skin
independence_class: independent GEO study; non-cancer disease context
current_status: fibroblast candidates and final transfer outputs available locally
biological_question: Does the inferred fibroblast plasticity axis separate keloid from normal scar/skin?
endpoint: Disease versus control pseudobulk composition, module score, and external transfer diagnostics.
limitations: Strong local signal but still observational; claims should remain composition/gradient, not mechanism or therapy.
recommended_next_action: Use as the stronger scar validation after GSE156326, with disease-control statistics and state-gradient figure source data.

disease_context: keloid versus normal scar/skin reference source
independence_class: independent public dataset, but not independent if used to derive scar programs
current_status: local raw/meta and FibroDynMix project outputs available
biological_question: Can a reference FibroDynMix scar-state basis be learned and transferred to other scar datasets?
endpoint: Reference fit, state definitions, and transfer basis.
limitations: If used to define programs, it cannot also serve as independent validation for those programs.
recommended_next_action: Use either as discovery/reference or exclude from validation claims; document the choice before manuscript drafting.

independence_class: independent bulk fibroblast dataset; not single-cell composition validation
biological_question: Do FibroDynMix-linked programs have external expression support in sorted fibroblast subsets?
limitations: Only two sorted profiles in the current summary; descriptive support only, not inferential validation.
recommended_next_action: Use as supplementary external endpoint support, not as the minimum independent scRNA-seq validation.

independence_class: independent GEO study; limited control structure in current local summary
current_status: fibroblast candidates and final transfer outputs available locally
biological_question: Is the inferred scar-state axis recoverable in an additional keloid-context dataset?
limitations: Current summary has disease samples without matched control rows, so endpoint evidence is incomplete.
recommended_next_action: Keep as supplementary transfer stability evidence unless control labels are curated.

independence_class: independent GEO study; two-condition scar comparison
current_status: fibroblast candidates, project outputs, and separate FibroDynMix workflow available locally
biological_question: Can the state axis distinguish keloid from hypertrophic scar in a compact independent dataset?
endpoint: Transfer stability and keloid-versus-hypertrophic composition contrast.
limitations: Small two-sample structure limits statistical claims.
recommended_next_action: Use as sensitivity/supporting panel after GSE156326 and GSE181316.

disease_context: mouse normal mammary fibroblast versus CAF
independence_class: independent public record but non-human and limited condition design
current_status: already analyzed in multi-public real-data validation
biological_question: Can the raw-count workflow execute on an additional public fibroblast count source?
endpoint: Technical public-data transfer and state composition.
limitations: Non-human and narrow source; weak support for an iScience human disease-atlas claim.
recommended_next_action: Retain as technical reproducibility evidence, not a central biological validation.
